title: Unleashing entrepreneurial ingenuity (1)
sub_topics: Martin Luther King Jr. quotation on jobs/liberty/happiness
Confucius — teach a man to fish
Global Forum on Liberty and Prosperity (October 18-20, 2006)
75th birthday founding of Foundation for Liberty and Prosperity (2011)
US industrial pioneers (Edison, Tesla, Vanderbilt, Rockefeller, Carnegie, Ford, Morgan)
Roosevelt and Eisenhower as good-governance leaders

stances.claim: Truth is eternal and limitless — not bound by sovereignty, territory, ideology, or legality; and this universal truth is that humans need both justice and jobs, freedom and food, ethics and economics, peace and development, liberty and prosperity.
The best way to conquer poverty, create wealth, and share prosperity is to unleash the entrepreneurial genius of people by granting them the freedom and the tools to help themselves and society.

The role of government and law is to provide guarantees and incentives, create supporting institutions, and promulgate minimal regulations that prevent monopolization and ensure obligations like reasonable taxes — government inspires shared prosperity, not directs it.
stances.rhetorical_move: minimal-regulation framing of state role within liberty-prosperity philosophy

stances.claim: The thesis is validated worldwide — US affluence came from pioneers who defied monarchical tyrannies and unleashed inventors; China's modern prosperity came from Deng Xiaoping unleashing entrepreneurial ingenuity under One-Country-Two-Systems; South Korea rose on private initiative while North Korea wallows in poverty from its grip on creativity.
stances.rhetorical_move: three-country comparative validation tour as evidentiary structure

stances.claim: Human arrogance, greed, and avarice lurk in all undertakings, but they are the exception rather than the rule — 'we must never stop dreaming for fear that reality may shatter our dreams.'
stances.rhetorical_move: philosophical reply to caution + aphoristic closer

institutions: Global Forum on Liberty and Prosperity
events: Global Forum on Liberty and Prosperity (October 18-20, 2006)
Founding of FLP at his 75th birthday (2011)

justice and jobs; freedom and food; ethics and economics; peace and development; liberty and prosperity
these twin beacons must always go together; one is useless without the other
unleash the entrepreneurial genius of people
Give a man a fish and you feed him for a day. Teach him to fish and you feed him for a lifetime
we must never stop dreaming for fear that reality may shatter our dreams
the twin anchors of my being as a lawyer and as a jurist
register_markers: Let me take you briefly around the world
decision_framework_signals: applies 'twin beacons' framework as the unifying lens across legal, economic, and political development
uses fisherman parable as didactic device for the entrepreneurial-ingenuity thesis
uses three-country comparative validation (US/China/Korea) as evidentiary structure for universalist claims
frames role of government as minimal-but-inspirational rather than directive
notable_anecdotes: Convening the Global Forum on Liberty and Prosperity at Makati Shangri-la on October 18-20, 2006 as his valedictory activity as Chief Justice
